summary: The author compiled a curated list of high-scoring solutions from a previous LMSYS Chatbot Arena competition. The post serves as a reference guide, linking to the top-ranked submissions to help participants get started. It does not present a new technical approach or detailed methodology.
extensive_summary:
This post functions as a curated directory rather than a technical writeup. The author aggregates links to the highest-ranking solutions from a prior iteration of the LMSYS Chatbot Arena competition. By organizing them by rank (1st through 26th place), the post provides a quick starting point for competitors looking to study past methodologies.

No specific data pipelines, model architectures, or training strategies are detailed within this text itself. Instead, it acts as a meta-resource, pointing readers to external discussion threads where the actual technical breakdowns, code, and insights are hosted.

other_solution_refs.description: 1st Place Solution: Distill is all you need
13th Place Solution: Task-Domain Adaptation, Prompting and Focal Loss
16th Place - So Close to Gold Medal
21st place - Pseudo Labels, Feature Engineering and stable validation scores
22nd solution: base model, training, and post-processing
26th Solution for LMSYS - Chatbot Arena Human Preference Predictions
